name: axcelerator
description: Language Model Tools that let GitHub Copilot drive Microsoft Excel via xlwings (and COM on Windows): workbooks, sheets, ranges, formulas, tables, charts, Power Query, PivotTables, VBA, and Python.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In an untrusted workspace, tools that execute code or fetch data are blocked: excel_run_vba, excel_run_python, excel_add_power_query, and excel_refresh.

axcelerator.pythonPath.description: Path to the Python interpreter that has xlwings installed.

axcelerator.requestTimeoutMs.description: Maximum time (ms) to wait for an Excel operation to complete.

axcelerator.allowVba.description: Allow Copilot to run arbitrary VBA macros (Windows only). Off by default for safety.

axcelerator.allowPython.description: Allow Copilot to run arbitrary Python (xlwings) snippets. Off by default for safety.

languageModelTools.name: excel_check_environment
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reports whether Microsoft Excel and xlwings are available, the OS, Excel version, and per-capability flags. Windows (COM): live VBA, live PivotTables, direct Power Query API. macOS: Power Query via xlsx ZIP string-patching (definition-only; user must click Load), PivotTables via static summarised table (no PivotCache, no refresh), VBA unsupported. Inspect `powerQueryMode` and `pivotTableMode` in the result before planning. Call this first when unsure.

languageModelTools.name: excel_list_workbooks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all workbooks currently open in Excel along with their full paths and active sheet.

languageModelTools.name: excel_open_workbook
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens an existing workbook from disk, or creates a new empty workbook if `path` is omitted or the file does not exist (when `create=true`). Returns the workbook name.

path.description: Absolute path to the .xlsx/.xlsm file. Omit to create an unsaved new workbook.

create.description: If true and the file does not exist, create it.

languageModelTools.name: excel_save_workbook
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Saves a workbook. If `path` is provided, performs a Save As to that path.

workbook.description: Workbook name (e.g. 'Book1.xlsx') or full path. Required.

languageModelTools.name: excel_close_workbook
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Closes a workbook. Set `save=true` to save first.

languageModelTools.name: excel_list_sheets
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists worksheets in a workbook with their used range dimensions.

languageModelTools.name: excel_add_sheet
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Adds a new worksheet to a workbook.

after.description: Name of the sheet to insert after. Optional.

languageModelTools.name: excel_delete_sheet
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Deletes a worksheet from a workbook.

languageModelTools.name: excel_read_range
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads values from a range. Returns a 2D array. Use `expand='table'` to auto-detect the populated region starting at `range`.

languageModelTools.name: excel_write_range
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Writes values into a range. `values` is a 2D array (rows of columns). The range anchor cell is `range`; size is inferred from `values`.

languageModelTools.name: excel_set_formula
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sets one or more Excel formulas. If `formula` is a string, it is applied to the anchor cell. If `formulas` is provided (2D array), it fills a region. Use `array=true` for dynamic-array formulas.

languageModelTools.name: excel_format_range
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Applies basic formatting to a range: number format, bold, italic, font color, background color, column width, autofit.

languageModelTools.name: excel_create_table
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Converts a range into an Excel Table (ListObject). Required for many dashboard/PivotTable scenarios.

languageModelTools.name: excel_create_chart
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a chart on a sheet from a source range.

anchorCell.description: Top-left anchor cell for the chart, e.g. 'F2'.

languageModelTools.name: excel_create_pivot_table
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a PivotTable from an Excel Table or range. WINDOWS: builds a real PivotTable via the COM PivotCache (interactive, refreshable). macOS: writes a STATIC summarised table at the destination cell using Python aggregation — it is NOT a live PivotTable, has no PivotCache, and does not refresh when the source changes. Use check_environment.pivotTableMode to see which path will run.

sourceTable.description: Name of an existing Excel Table to use as source.

languageModelTools.name: excel_add_power_query
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Adds (or replaces) a Power Query connection defined by an M-expression. WINDOWS: full COM automation, including loading the query as a Query Table on a sheet (loadToSheet/loadToCell). macOS: the workbook is saved, closed, the .xlsx package is patched with the DataMashup blob, and reopened; the query becomes visible in the Power Query editor but is NOT auto-loaded to a sheet (loadToSheet and loadToCell are ignored on Mac — the user must click Load in the PQ editor). Requires a trusted workspace (M can fetch from web/file/SharePoint).

mFormula.description: Full Power Query M expression, including 'let ... in ...'.

loadToSheet.description: Windows only: loads the query as a Query Table to this sheet. IGNORED on macOS — the query is visible in the PQ editor but the user must click Load.

loadToCell.description: Windows only: target cell for loadToSheet. Ignored on macOS.

noReopen.description: Mac only: after patching the .xlsx package on disk, do not reopen the workbook in Excel. Use this when subsequent xlwings saves would otherwise strip the injected DataMashup.

languageModelTools.name: excel_refresh
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Refreshes all data connections and Power Queries in a workbook (or a specific query if `queryName` is given). WINDOWS: per-connection refresh with error propagation. macOS: best-effort; queryName is ignored, the call asks Excel to refresh all and errors raised inside the Excel UI are not surfaced. Requires a trusted workspace.

languageModelTools.name: excel_run_vba
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a VBA macro by name in the given workbook, optionally with arguments. WINDOWS-ONLY (requires the Excel COM object model). On macOS the call returns an unsupported error — use excel_run_python instead. Disabled unless `axcelerator.allowVba` is enabled and the workspace is trusted.

languageModelTools.name: excel_run_python
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs an arbitrary Python snippet against the live Excel session via xlwings. The snippet has `xw` (xlwings module), `app` (active App), and `wb` (target workbook, if `workbook` provided) in scope. Assign the result to a variable named `result` to return it. Disabled unless `axcelerator.allowPython` is enabled.

code.description: Python source. Set `result = ...` to return a value.
